basic_info.title: Grandia II
basic_info.review: Grandia II offers a charming and engaging JRPG experience with a unique battle system and beautiful world, though it suffers from some technical limitations on the Dreamcast.

play_today.details: Widely considered the definitive original experience by the community. Features superior graphics, smoother frame rates, and no slowdown compared to the PS2 port. The North American version includes official English localization. Requires hardware or emulation (KallistiOS) to play today, offering the most faithful representation of the game's intended design and audio quality.

recommended_patches.name: Grandia II UNDUB Patch
recommended_patches.description: Restores the original Japanese voice acting to the North American Dreamcast release and improves the English translation quality, replacing the controversial Ubisoft dub.

recommended_patches.name: Grandia II Widescreen + 60FPS Battle Hack
recommended_patches.description: A hex patch that enables 16:9 anamorphic widescreen mode and forces combat battles to run at a stable 60 frames per second on the Dreamcast.

optimal_settings: Enable texture filtering (Bilinear) for smoother sprite scaling
Set internal resolution to 2x or 3x native for sharper visuals
Use accurate audio emulation to preserve original sound quality
Enable 'Widescreen' cheat code in the emulator's cheat menu
Enable 'True Anamorphic Widescreen' in Emulation Hacks for 16:9 support
Set internal resolution to 2x or 4x for modern display compatibility
Use 'Direct3D 11' renderer for best performance on modern GPUs
Enable '60 FPS' cheat code for smoother combat sequences
Enable texture filtering for better visual clarity on mobile screens
Set resolution to 2x native for a balance of performance and quality
Configure on-screen controls to overlay transparently for better visibility

play_today.details: HD Remaster version with enhanced visuals, widescreen support, and multiple language options. Based on Anniversary Edition code with additional fixes.

recommended_patches.name: HD Remaster Bug Fix Patch
recommended_patches.description: Official patch addressing various bugs including localization errors and game freezing issues (version 1.01.00+)

play_today.details: Enhanced PC port with visual upgrades, Japanese voice option, and additional difficulty level. Includes Steam achievements and cloud saves.

recommended_patches.name: HQ Music Mod
recommended_patches.description: Replaces up to 130 sound files with higher quality 44.1kHz audio for improved music fidelity

play_today.details: Generally regarded as the worst version due to significant graphical downgrades, texture issues, and noticeable slowdown during combat. However, it remains a viable option for those with existing PS2 hardware who cannot access Dreamcast or PC. The North American version offers official English text. Not recommended for new players due to technical inferiority compared to other versions.

optimal_settings: Set internal resolution to 2x or 4x to compensate for the PS2's lower native resolution
Enable 'Texture Filtering' (Bilinear) to smooth out jagged edges
Use 'GSdx' plugin with 'Hardware' renderer for best performance
Enable 'Patch' support to apply widescreen codes if available

play_today.details: The most convenient modern option via Steam, featuring native widescreen support, controller remapping, and cloud saves. Includes the 'Hard Mode' and Japanese audio track not found in original releases. While early patches were buggy, recent updates have stabilized performance. Best for accessibility and QoL features, though purists may prefer the Dreamcast's raw feel.

recommended_patches.name: Grandia II HD Remaster - Various Fixes
recommended_patches.description: Community-created patch for the Steam version that fixes texture glitches, incorrect dialog displays, and other bugs present in the official HD Remaster release.

description.synopsis: Grandia II follows Ryudo, a young boy seeking revenge against the evil organization that killed his parents. Alongside his friends, he uncovers a conspiracy involving the mysterious 'Grandia' and the true nature of evil. The story explores themes of redemption, friendship, and the power of music in a world filled with magic and adventure.
key_features: Active Time Battle (ATB) system with real-time movement
Unique 'Grandia' battle system allowing character positioning
Character-specific skill trees and weapon mastery
Dynamic camera system with manual control
Extensive side quests and hidden dungeons
Character customization through equipment and accessories
Seamless transition between exploration and combat
legacy_and_impact: Grandia II is remembered as a cult classic JRPG that pushed the boundaries of the genre on the Dreamcast. Its unique battle system and open-world exploration influenced later RPGs, and it remains a beloved title among fans of the series.
